key_points: Mold, rats, and trash reported inside multiple dorms
Hurricane damage worsened already deteriorating conditions
Newer dorms exist but older buildings still actively used
key_quotes: Show them the mold infested buildings. Show them the rat infested buildings.
This is how it USED to look, bcuz it don't look like that now!
Huh not too much has changed with Bronson Hall/Annex, retiling expected with the old style for the community bathrooms.

key_points: Homecoming is vibrant with Greek step shows and tailgates
Greek life dominates social culture, non-D9 students can feel excluded
Beach proximity and Daytona location add unique social options
key_quotes: Appreciate this video. I only attended my HBCU homecoming and wondered what others was like, especially BCU and Howard U.
Homecoming 2019 was great being on the yard.
Dang it's so much different from when I was there.

key_points: Former students explicitly warn against buying a meal plan
No detailed reviews of food quality in available data
Buying groceries recommended over the mandatory meal plan
key_quotes: Do Not get a meal plan!!! Buy a grocery store plan.
Nobody on Youtube talks about the tuition fees... they mentioned everything else but tuition, dorms, and meal plan because they know the cost will scare students away.

key_points: Students accepting poor conditions normalizes low expectations, per observers
Homeless people reportedly walking through campus adds stress and safety concerns
No formal mental health support resources mentioned by students
key_quotes: The students accepting those conditions as young adults will not prepare them for how to expect the best for themselves.
Security is one of the things Ed spoke about as he stated the homeless come through the campus all the time.
Those kids are not asking for much. Living in those types of conditions will make them use to bullshit.

key_points: Tuition jumped from roughly $19K to $23-28K in recent years
Scholarships available for band and athletes but limited otherwise
Questions raised about where $60M+ in COVID and state funds actually went
key_quotes: When I came in it was like 19k... Paying for band is trash.. I played in the band so I didn't pay a lot my scholarship almost covered everything.
Nobody on Youtube talks about the tuition fees... they know the cost will scare students away.

In addition to the over 60 million Bethune-Cookman received, the Florida Governor gave $16 million for their infrastructure, and the Intern President said he has spent a quarter of a million to fix things. Where's the stash, Bethune?

key_points: CS and Music programs mentioned but quality details are scarce
Credit transfer problems reported — take this seriously before enrolling
Low national profile raises visa and reputation concerns for international students
key_quotes: Credits don't always transfer from college to college. The kids need to make sure they take classes that aren't a waste.
I'm thinking about committing for this fall but I'm worried about not being able to transfer if I don't enjoy it, I was hearing stuff about BCU not transferring credits.
BCU was at the bottom of my list but I'm glad someone accepted me.

key_points: Students report not seeing their president for over a year
Board ousted a reform-minded president who demanded accountability
Alumni funds raised but never accounted for — transparency is absent
key_quotes: They've not seen school President since last year.
Dr. Crite was wonderful... they ran him off because he 'wanted to know too much.' He demanded excellence and change, the board didn't want that.
School told Alumni Association raised over 1/4mil for school but never told how it was spent. They were told to step back.

key_points: Campus exterior is beautiful but interiors are reportedly neglected
Daytona Beach location is appealing but brings safety concerns
Campus tours accused of only showing cleaned exterior areas
key_quotes: They mustive just showed the good parts bcuz students say the bad parts been there for years. so this is obviously selected viewing. they didnt go inside not one building.
Show them the buildings full of trash. Show them the mold infested buildings.
I still see the campus is extremely beautiful each year that I returned.

key_points: No specific job placement or career outcome data from students
Basketball gym needs renovation, limiting athletic recruits and visibility
New grad program is available but reputation concerns linger
key_quotes: I wish BCC now BCU would invest in their basketball program... The gym needs to be renovated to attract more recruits.
BCU is a great school.
BCU was at the bottom of my list but I'm glad someone accepted me.

key_points: Paying $28K/year for mold, rats, and absent leadership is widely criticized
Students feel the experience does not match the price tag at all
Massive external funding received but campus conditions remain poor
key_quotes: I'm so pissed that our own people turn a blind eye for the $. These kids deserve better for what they paid for 31k a year for each student.
You gave a fair assessment... Those kids are not asking for much.
So currently I'm a junior, and really wanted to go to Bethune for music. So should I reconsider it?

key_points: Strong alumni love for BCU's legacy but deep anger at current leadership
Students are resilient and community-oriented despite institutional failures
National scrutiny from Ed Reed and Roland Martin forced rare public accountability
key_quotes: Alums have been saying this for years. We will not be silenced.
Thank you Roland for hosting this town hall and giving a voice to all those who have been silenced for so long.
I've been hearing so many bad things so I'm super nervous!

key_points: **Safety**: Homeless individuals regularly entering campus with little security response
**Facilities**: Mold, rodents, and trash inside dorms confirmed by multiple independent reporters
**Financial accountability**: $60M+ in COVID and state funds received, campus conditions unchanged, no public accounting
key_quotes: Show them the mold infested buildings. Show them the rat infested buildings. Don't whistle past the graveyard and not show the buried skeletons.
Where is the money going.... Also, fill in the gaps with why the board doesn't present itself... almost an additional $68Mil... Appears that an audit is in order.
They've not seen school President since last year. School told Alumni Association raised over 1/4mil for school but never told how it was spent.

key_points: Band scholarships can make attendance nearly free for musicians
Mary McLeod Bethune is buried on campus — a uniquely powerful historical site
Daytona Beach setting offers beach access and warm weather year-round
key_quotes: I played in the band so I didn't pay a lot my scholarship almost covered everything.
